title: Whole-Country Enacted Budget Law Totals: Revenue & Expenditure (FY1371-1401)
time_range: FY1371-1401 (1992-2023 Western fiscal-year-end; 30 of 31 possible fiscal years, FY1395 confirmed missing)
notes: Direct year-continuation of majlis__national_budget_totals_by_fiscal_year (which itself extends wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL/GC.REV.XGRT through FY1370) -- this chart picks up at exactly FY1371, one fiscal year after the Majlis series ends, from a separately-sourced raw folder (Iran Data Portal, not lamtakam.com). Same IMPORTANT CONCEPT CAVEAT applies: these are ENACTED-LAW (ex-ante) figures from the law's own single-article clause, NOT audited outturns -- WDI's GC.* series is outturn/actuals-based, so years where both exist should be read as two different vintages of the same fiscal year, not cross-validated or merged here. Jointly extends wdi__GC.XPN.TOTL (expenditure_rials, primary) and wdi__GC.REV.XGRT (revenue_rials, co-extended -- revenue=expenditure in every row, the law sets a balanced budget by design). Also carries internal Part A (general govt)/Part A1 (general revenue)/Part A2 (earmarked revenue)/Part B (state companies+banks) breakdown not present in WDI's single aggregate. STRUCTURAL FINDING: in every year both Part A and Part B were captured (~20 years), A+B numerically EXCEEDS the printed Grand Total -- confirmed as a persistent, unexplained feature of Iranian budget-law drafting (first flagged for FY1368/1370 in the Majlis series), not a transcription error; kept as printed throughout, never reconciled. Two fiscal years (FY1397, FY1398) carry TWO distinct figures each (bill-stage vs. final-enacted-law), both kept as separate rows in the underlying CSV, neither treated as authoritative. FY1395 is a confirmed missing source (no PDF exists in the raw folder for that year), not a legibility failure. Full caveats: data/processed/iran_plan_budget_org_series/README.md.

title: Five-Year Development Plan Headline GDP Growth Targets (Plans 2, 6, 7)
time_range: FY1374-1406 (1995/96-2027/28, non-continuous: Plan 2 [1374-78], Plan 6 [1396-1400], Plan 7 BILL [1402-1406]; Plans 3-5 not located)
notes: A POLICY-TARGET series, not a measured-outcome series -- structurally distinct from every GDP-growth chart already in the registry (WDI, Maddison, OWID all measure realized/estimated growth; this measures what the law/bill SET AS THE TARGET for the coming five years). No existing chart_id in the registry covers Iranian development-plan targets at all. Plan 2 (FY1374-78) targets 5.1% avg annual GDP growth (constant 1361 prices); Plan 6 (FY1396-1400) and Plan 7 (BILL, FY1402-1406, not yet enacted at collection time) both target 8% avg annual growth, alongside a target Gini coefficient of 0.34 by Plan 6's final year -- an unusual instance of an explicit inequality target inside a growth-plan law. Plans 1, 3, 4, 5's headline growth targets were NOT located within this extraction pass (Plan 1 delegates to an unopened appendix; Plans 3-5 are organized as policy-reform chapters without a compact targets clause) -- genuine gaps, flagged not filled. A natural future chart pairing: overlay this against WDI's actual Iran GDP-growth series (wdi__NY.GDP.MKTP.KD.ZG or equivalent) to visualize target-vs-outcome, once that overlay work is done -- not attempted here, this staging entry is data landing only.

title: Five-Year Development Plan Sectoral Value-Added & Employment Growth Targets (Plans 6-7)
notes: 9-sector (agriculture, oil, mining, industry, water/power/gas, construction, transport/storage, communications, other services) average-annual value-added growth AND employment growth targets, Plan 6 (enacted law) plus Plan 7's comparable sector-growth targets (BILL). No existing chart_id covers sector-level GROWTH TARGETS for Iran (WDI's NV.* sector value-added series are realized levels, not targets). Widest target seen: Communications 19.4% avg annual value-added growth in Plan 6, by far the highest of any sector, consistent with Iran's stated post-JCPOA push to diversify from oil. Plan 7's bill omits a directly-labeled 'other services'-style residual breakdown identical to Plan 6's table structure in the sampled pages -- treat the two plans' sector lists as close but not guaranteed byte-identical categories.

title: Five-Year Development Plan Investment & Credit Ceilings by Year (Plans 1-2)
notes: Annual investment-credit / bank-facility ceilings stated directly in each plan's own ماده واحده clause -- a distinct policy-instrument concept (a legislated spending CEILING by year) rather than a realized-spending level, so classified new rather than as an extension of any existing investment/capital-formation chart. Plan 1's figures are total 5-year program envelopes split by funding source (general revenue vs. state-company internal resources vs. current/operating spending) plus a separate annual bank-facility ceiling table (FY1368: 1,210bn rials through FY1371: 623bn rials, FY1372 blank in source). Plan 2's figures are a single annual ceiling table from general-revenue budget (FY1374: 36,394bn rials rising to FY1378: 60,825bn rials) -- a much larger nominal scale than Plan 1's five years earlier, consistent with this database's broader finding of rapid nominal budget growth across the 1990s (see iranplanbudgetorg__annual_budget_law_totals for the same-era whole-country totals).
